title: MQTT QoS-1 Secure Event Logger
design_question: How to guarantee access events reach the broker even under brief WiFi instability?
selected_approach: MQTT QoS 1 (at-least-once) with local buffer in ESP32 PSRAM. Events include: UID, timestamp, action (ALLOW/DENY), device_id.
rationale: QoS 1 re-transmits until broker ACKs—prevents silent event loss. Local buffer stores events during WiFi drop (up to 1000 events).

alternatives.reason_rejected: Higher overhead; not easily buffered locally; MQTT preferred for IoT messaging—rejected.

verification_notes: FACT: 'MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data' confirms QoS 1 at-least-once delivery semantics.
supporting_fact_titles: MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data
NTP Time Synchronisation for Data Logging
